description:
## Parent

bsm-7en

## What to build

Extend the existing real desktop WebDriver Issue List suite to prove the completed Issue Detail path end to end. The suite should launch the built desktop app against a disposable Beadwork workspace, click an Issue row, and verify selected Issue Detail content renders through the full Rust adapter → TauRPC → Effect → React path. Update stale WebDriver/E2E documentation and summary-oriented references.

This is the final hardening slice. Earlier slices should include unit and component/integration tests where possible; this slice focuses on the real desktop proof.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] The existing Issue List WebDriver fixture includes description and comments suitable for detail-rendering assertions.
- [ ] The WebDriver success scenario clicks an Issue row and verifies representative Issue Detail content renders in the real desktop app.
- [ ] The E2E test stays focused on user-visible outcomes and avoids over-asserting visual implementation details.
- [ ] Existing empty-list E2E behavior continues to pass.
- [ ] WebDriver/E2E documentation is updated for the selected Issue Detail path.
- [ ] Stale `IssueSummary` / `list_issue_summaries` references in relevant docs are updated to `Issue` / `list_issues` language.

## Blocked by

bsm-7en.2
bsm-7en.3
bsm-7en.4
bsm-7en.5
title: Extend desktop E2E coverage for issue detail
